findings:
---
finding_type: audience_insight
summary: Buyers want a fast way to compare tiers without reading feature grids line by line
source_ids: src_01, src_02
confidence: high
evidence_note: Matches the selected short-form teardown angle
---
---
finding_type: audience_insight
summary: Pricing anxiety spikes when the premium tier looks expensive before its context is clear
source_ids: src_02
confidence: medium
evidence_note: Strong directional support
---
---
finding_type: competitor_observation
summary: Strong performers explain the upgrade trigger before they explain every feature
source_ids: src_03
confidence: medium
evidence_note: Useful contrast with weaker competitor patterns
---
---
finding_type: example
summary: A pricing page that surfaces the premium tier outcome before the checklist
source_ids: src_04
confidence: medium
evidence_note: Concrete visual example for the script
---
---
finding_type: case_study
summary: A SaaS founder reduced discount requests after clarifying plan contrast
source_ids: src_05
confidence: low
evidence_note: Anecdotal but directionally useful
---
---
finding_type: gap_to_exploit
summary: Few creators explain pricing psychology with concrete page examples
source_ids: src_03, src_04
confidence: high
evidence_note: Clear whitespace in current creator content
---

claims:
---
claim_type: key_fact
claim: Anchoring changes perceived value when the higher-price option is framed first
source_ids: src_01
confidence: high
mechanism: Buyers compare relative value before they inspect every feature
---
---
claim_type: key_fact
claim: Buyers compare relative differences more than absolute feature counts
source_ids: src_01, src_02
confidence: medium
mechanism: Relative contrast simplifies fast evaluation
---
---
claim_type: proof_point
claim: The decoy effect is strongest when one tier makes the target tier feel more reasonable
source_ids: src_01
confidence: high
mechanism: The comparison set changes what feels expensive
---
---
claim_type: proof_point
claim: Order and framing influence willingness to pay before detailed evaluation
source_ids: src_02, src_03
confidence: high
mechanism: The first comparison anchors the rest of the page
---

counterpoints:
---
summary: Pricing psychology alone does not guarantee better conversion if plan positioning is unclear
why_it_matters: The script should avoid overclaiming impact from one page change
source_ids: src_05
confidence: medium
---

assets_needed:
- Screenshot of a three-tier pricing page
- Simple annotated mock showing anchor placement

uncertainty_flags:
---
flag_type: verification_required
claim: Any quantified lift claim tied to pricing page changes
reason: The available evidence is directional and context-sensitive
severity: medium
source_ids: src_05
---
---
flag_type: unsafe_or_uncertain
claim: Do not imply anchoring guarantees revenue growth
reason: The current source mix does not justify universal causal claims
severity: high
source_ids: src_05
---

research_stop_reason: Enough evidence to build a practical teardown without hard performance claims
